studies.title: Defining cardiac functional recovery in end-stage heart failure at single cell resolution
studies.overall_design: Single-nucleus RNA-seq of human heart tissue from heart failure patients pre- and post-LVAD (responders vs non-responders) and non-diseased donors; paired longitudinal sampling where applicable
studies.tissue: Heart (cardiac tissue)
studies.study_cohorts: Heart failure patients who recovered LV systolic function after LVAD (responders), heart failure patients who did not recover (non-responders), and non-diseased donor hearts; pre- and post-LVAD timepoints
studies.ejection_fraction_info: Recovery of LV systolic function after LVAD (responders vs non-responders); no specific EF values reported
studies.clinical_endpoint: Cardiac functional recovery (LV systolic function) and cell type–specific transcriptional signatures
studies.treatment_type: Surgical/mechanical circulatory support (LVAD implantation)
studies.data_availability: Available in GEO
studies.author: Kory Lavine
studies.orthogonal_validation: Mouse model of cardiac recovery mediated by BRD4 inhibition showed decreased macrophage and fibroblast Runx1 expression, reduced chromatin accessibility within a Runx1 intronic peak, and acquisition of human recovery signatures

studies.title: Altered Cardiac Energetics and Mitochondrial Dysfunction in Hypertrophic Cardiomyopathy
studies.overall_design: Comprehensive multi-omics profiling of human myocardial tissue comparing HCM patients and controls: untargeted/targeted metabolomics and lipidomics, bulk RNA sequencing, ultrastructural analyses by transmission EM and electron tomography, and mitochondrial functional assays (high-resolution respirometry, enzymatic activities). Samples included LV septal myectomy tissue from HCM patients and LV tissue from donor hearts; additional mitral stenosis patients served as controls for mitochondrial respiration assays. Analyses integrated pathway enrichment and network mapping to link transcriptional changes with metabolite/lipid alterations and mitochondrial structure/function
studies.tissue: Left ventricle myocardium (septal myectomy tissue from HCM; LV/interventricular septum/apex tissue from donor hearts)
studies.sample_collection_method: Surgical LV septal myectomy for HCM; myocardial tissue procurement from normal donor hearts; LV free wall samples from patients with primary mitral stenosis undergoing valve repair (for respiration assays)
studies.study_cohorts: - HCM: 27 patients undergoing LV septal myectomy (EF ≥55% at inclusion) - Healthy controls: 13 donor hearts with no cardiac history - Additional control disease group: 2 patients with primary mitral stenosis used only for mitochondrial respiration assays Total subjects in cohort table: 42
studies.ejection_fraction_info: Inclusion required EF ≥55% for HCM; HCM EF 67±7%; donor controls EF 58±6%; mitral stenosis controls EF 62±6%
studies.clinical_endpoint: Comparative profiling of cardiac energetics and mitochondrial structure/function between HCM and controls, including differential gene expression, metabolite/lipid alterations, mitochondrial respiration capacity, and ultrastructural damage (cristae density)
studies.publication_title: Altered Cardiac Energetics and Mitochondrial Dysfunction in Hypertrophic Cardiomyopathy
studies.data_availability: RNA-Seq data available in GEO (GSE180313); other study data available from the corresponding author upon reasonable request
studies.author: Sara Ranjbarvaziri; corresponding author Daniel Bernstein
studies.orthogonal_validation: - Transmission electron microscopy and 3D electron tomography for mitochondrial ultrastructure and cristae density - High-resolution respirometry (Oroboros) and spectrophotometric assays of mitochondrial complexes and citrate synthase activity - Western blots for 4-HNE (oxidative stress) and LC3A/B (autophagy) - qPCR for mtDNA:nDNA ratio - ELISA for pAMPK:total AMPK - Enzymatic activity assay for glutathione peroxidase (GPx1)

studies.title: Human Hypertrophic Cardiomyopathy mRNA Profiling
studies.overall_design: Cardiac tissue was collected from obstructive HCM patients (myectomy samples) and control cardiac tissues; RNA-sequencing was performed on all samples (Illumina NovaSeq 6000). A total of 97 HCM cases and 23 controls were included; proteomics and phosphoproteomics were performed on a representative subset for multi-omics comparisons, including sex-specific analyses
studies.tissue: Heart muscle (cardiac tissue; surgical myectomy specimens)
studies.sample_collection_method: Surgical myectomy and cardiac tissue biopsy
studies.study_cohorts: HCM cases (n=97; 53 males/44 females) and control cardiac tissues (n=23)
studies.clinical_endpoint: Sex-specific and disease-related gene expression differences in HCM versus control heart tissue (transcriptome/proteome/phosphoproteome comparisons)
studies.publication_title: A multi-omics atlas of sex-specific differences in obstructive hypertrophic cardiomyopathy
studies.data_availability: Available in GEO
studies.author: Michael J. Ackerman

studies.title: Multi-level transcriptome sequencing identifies COL1A1 as a candidate marker in human heart failure progression
studies.overall_design: Prospective follow-up study generating multi-level transcriptomic data (whole-transcriptome mRNA/lncRNA sequencing and small RNA-seq) from left ventricular tissue of 21 heart failure (HF) patients and 9 healthy donors. Fibrosis percentage per sample was quantified by Masson trichrome staining. A lasso regression model was applied to identify fibrosis- and progression-associated genes. Findings were validated by immunohistochemistry (IHC) in the same cohort, qRT-PCR in an independent cohort (20 HF and 9 healthy donors), and plasma ELISA for COL1A1 in a validation HF cohort (n=139) to assess prediction of progression to heart transplantation (HTx). Library preparation and sequencing were performed on Illumina HiSeq 2500; detailed RNA extraction, library prep, and sequencing protocols are provided
studies.tissue: Left ventricular heart tissue (LV)
studies.sample_collection_method: LV tissue obtained at time of heart transplantation (HTx) from HF patients; healthy LV samples from brain-death donors not suitable for transplantation; tissues preserved in liquid nitrogen
studies.study_cohorts: - Discovery cohort: 21 HF patients and 9 healthy donors for whole-transcriptome (mRNA/lncRNA) and small RNA sequencing. - IHC validation: 21 HF and 6 normal samples from the same cohort (availability-limited). - Independent qRT-PCR cohort: 20 HF and 9 normal controls. - Plasma validation cohort: 139 HF patients prior to HTx for ELISA-based COL1A1 measurement
studies.ejection_fraction_info: HF cohort: mean LVEF 27.6 ± 9.8%; 90.5% had reduced LVEF (<40%); one patient had preserved LVEF (≥50%); healthy donors required LVEF >60%
studies.clinical_endpoint: Time from initial symptoms and from HF onset to heart transplantation (HTx); 1-year HTx prediction using plasma COL1A1 level; myocardial fibrosis percentage quantified by Masson staining
studies.comorbidities: Diabetes mellitus; smoking history; pulmonary hypertension; tricuspid regurgitation; mitral regurgitation; arrhythmias (atrial fibrillation, premature ventricular contraction, ventricular tachycardia, ventricular fibrillation); CRBBB
studies.treatment_type: Pharmacological (standard medication treatment before HTx)
studies.treatment_agent_name: ASA; amiodarone; β-blocker; digoxin; ACEI/ARB; CCB; diuretic
studies.biological_replicates: yes (multiple independent patient samples per group)
studies.publication_title: Multi-level transcriptome sequencing identifies COL1A1 as a candidate marker in human heart failure progression
studies.data_availability: Available in GEO under accession GSE135055
studies.author: Xiumeng Hua
studies.orthogonal_validation: Immunohistochemistry (COL1A1 IHC) in LV tissue; quantitative real-time PCR in an independent cohort; plasma ELISA for COL1A1

studies.title: Regional Differences in mRNA and lncRNA Expression Profiles in Non- Failing Human Atria and Ventricles
studies.overall_design: RNA-Seq profiling of mRNA and lncRNA from paired transmural tissue samples collected from five regions (left and right atrial appendages, left and right ventricles, interventricular septum) of non-failing human donor hearts (N=8). Poly-A–selected libraries were prepared (TruSeq v2), sequenced as paired-end 2×101 bp on Illumina HiSeq 2500, and reads were aligned (TopHat) and quantified (HTSeq). Differential expression used paired analysis with edgeR (TMM normalization, FDR<0.05, fold-change ≥1.5); GO/KEGG enrichment and PCA/clustering were performed. Data deposited in GEO (GSE112339)
studies.tissue: Transmural cardiac tissue from left atrial appendage (LA), right atrial appendage (RA), left ventricle (LV), right ventricle (RV), and interventricular septum (IVS)
studies.sample_collection_method: Transmural tissue samples from donor hearts declined for transplantation were collected in the operating room, immediately flash-frozen in liquid nitrogen and stored at −80 °C
studies.study_cohorts: Non-failing adult donor hearts (N=8), five paired regions per heart (LA, RA, LV, RV, IVS); RNA-Seq cohort included 6 male and 2 female donors; separate validation cohort for RT-qPCR included 8 male and 8 female LA/RA samples
studies.clinical_endpoint: Regional gene expression profiling (mRNA and lncRNA) across human heart chambers; identification of chamber-enriched transcripts and pathways
studies.source: NCBI Gene Expression Omnibus (GEO); Washington University School of Medicine
studies.publication_title: Regional Differences in mRNA and lncRNA Expression Profiles in Non- Failing Human Atria and Ventricles
studies.data_availability: Available in GEO (GSE112339)
studies.author: Jeanne M. Nerbonne
studies.orthogonal_validation: Independent validation by quantitative RT-qPCR on separate LA/RA samples (8 male and 8 female)
studies.public_datasets_used_for_validation: Non-failing human LV RNA-Seq dataset GSE46224 (PMID 24429688); Medicalgenomics RNAseq Atlas (PMID 22345621; PMID 20668672)

studies.title: Plasma multi-omics identify early pathogenesis and biomarkers of SCD post-AMI
studies.overall_design: Fasting blood (5 mL) was collected within 24 hours after emergent admission in EDTA tubes from post-AMI patients. A total of 110 samples were obtained, comprising 55 patients who experienced sudden cardiac death (SCD) and 55 matched survivors. Peripheral blood mononuclear cells (PBMCs) were separated within 20 minutes after blood collection; total RNA was isolated using TRIzol reagent, incubated for 15 minutes at room temperature, and frozen at -80°C prior to extraction. RNA-Seq was performed on the collected samples
studies.tissue: Blood plasma; peripheral blood mononuclear cells (PBMCs)
studies.sample_collection_method: Fasting blood draw within 24 hours of emergent admission using EDTA tubes; PBMC separation within 20 minutes of collection
studies.study_cohorts: 55 pairs of post-AMI patients: SCD cases and matched survivors (controls)
studies.clinical_endpoint: Sudden Cardiac Death (SCD) after AMI; early prediction and biomarker identification; blood transcriptomic changes
studies.data_availability: Available in GEO
studies.author: Maomao Zhang
studies.orthogonal_validation: Targeted proteome quantification identified a TOP4 protein-based biomarker panel, validated in internal and external cohorts; additional interventions targeting complement factor D (CFD) suggested as a therapeutic strategy

studies.title: The Myocardial Infarction Platelet Transcriptome
studies.overall_design: Prospective cohort of women aged ≥21 referred for diagnostic coronary angiography, including acute MI cases and non-MI disease controls. Peripheral blood was drawn at the onset of angiography after clinically indicated aspirin and P2Y12 inhibitor administration. Platelets were isolated from citrate-anticoagulated blood, purified by leukocyte/RBC depletion, and RNA extracted. Barcoded libraries were prepared with SMART-Seq HT and paired-end sequenced on Illumina NovaSeq 6000. Reads were processed with Seq-N-Slide, aligned to hg38 with STAR, quantified with featureCounts, and differential expression analyzed with DESeq2. Pathway analyses (Ingenuity, Gene Ontology, Reactome) and singscore were used. A longitudinal subset of MI patients provided follow-up samples; an external PACE peripheral artery disease cohort (n=135) was used to assess association of chronically altered MI platelet genes with incident MACE
studies.disease: Myocardial infarction (including MICAD and MINOCA subtypes)
studies.tissue: Isolated platelets from peripheral blood
studies.sample_collection_method: Peripheral blood draw at onset of coronary angiography; blood collected into 3.2% sodium citrate tubes, processed immediately to platelet-rich plasma (200 g, 10 min), followed by platelet isolation/purification via microbead depletion of leukocytes/RBCs; RNA extracted with Direct-zol columns
studies.study_cohorts: - Women with acute MI (n=40) - Women with stable cardiac symptoms without MI as disease controls (n=38) - MI subtypes: MICAD (n=19) and MINOCA (n=21) - Longitudinal follow-up samples from 13 MI patients
studies.total_samples: 91 (40 MI baseline + 38 controls baseline + 13 MI follow-up)
studies.patient_sample_mapping_available: yes (GEO sample titles include HARP patient IDs with baseline/follow-up)
studies.clinical_endpoint: Primary: platelet gene expression differences between MI and controls and across MI subtypes; Secondary: association of chronically altered platelet genes with incident MACE (composite of death, MI, or stroke) in an external cohort
studies.comorbidities: Hypertension, diabetes mellitus, dyslipidemia, chronic kidney disease, current smoking; also depression and anxiety reported
studies.treatment_type: Pharmacological (standard-of-care antiplatelet therapy during clinical care)
studies.publication_title: Myocardial Infarction Platelet Gene Expression Signatures in Women
studies.data_availability: Sequencing data available on GSE272178 and GSE232027; additional data/results/code available upon request
studies.author: Tessa J. Barrett
studies.orthogonal_validation: Flow cytometry measurement of monocyte-platelet aggregates; routine blood leukocyte counts; plasma TNF-α concentration comparison between MICAD and MINOCA

studies.title: Systemic environment in acute myocardial infarction programs human macrophages for trauma repair
studies.overall_design: Primary human CD14+ monocytes from healthy donors were isolated from PBMCs, pooled (6–8 donors), and differentiated into macrophages with M-CSF. Fully differentiated macrophages were then exposed for 24 h to a 1:5 dilution of human serum collected at admission from AMI patients (POSTEMI cohort) or matched healthy controls, followed by bulk RNA sequencing and multiparameter high-content functional profiling (Macroscreen). The discovery cohort (POSTEMI STEMI) comprised AMI and control sera, enabling identification of AMI-induced macrophage transcriptional and functional changes and association with 4‑month cardiac MRI outcomes (EF, EDV, infarct size). Findings were validated in an independent AMI/control serum cohort by functional assays and against a public PBMC dataset; network-guided drug repurposing (LINCS L1000) was performed and PGE2 perturbation RNA-seq was used to test reversal of adverse programs
studies.tissue: Primary human monocyte-derived macrophages (from CD14+ monocytes); patient sera (blood)
studies.sample_collection_method: Blood collected at admission (peripheral venipuncture for NSTEMI/controls; arterial cannula for STEMI), processed to serum by coagulation and centrifugation; PBMC isolation from healthy donor buffy coats by Ficoll, CD14+ selection, and macrophage differentiation with M-CSF
studies.study_cohorts: Discovery cohort: 50 POSTEMI STEMI patients and 20 risk factor–matched healthy controls; AMI sera obtained median 2.8 h after symptom onset. POSTEMI subgrouping: 25 POSTEMI-Small and 25 POSTEMI-Large by 4‑month CMR infarct size/EF/EDV. Validation cohort: 47 AMI (23 STEMI, 24 NSTEMI) and 25 age-matched healthy controls. Public validation: PBMC dataset GSE59867 (AMI vs controls; HF vs non-HF subsets)
studies.ejection_fraction_info: Left ventricular EF (CMR at 4 months) was 64.8 ± 1.0% in POSTEMI-Small vs 36.1 ± 2.1% in POSTEMI-Large; EDV also differed (161.0 ± 30.4 mL vs 248 ± 12.2 mL). EF and EDV were used to assess ventricular dysfunction and prognosis
studies.clinical_endpoint: Cardiac function and remodeling at 4 months post-AMI assessed by CMR (left ventricular ejection fraction, end-diastolic volume, infarct size); association of macrophage gene programs with prognosis (ventricular dysfunction/HF risk)
studies.comorbidities: Hypertension; Diabetes; Smoking status reported in cohort characteristics
studies.treatment_type: Pharmacological in vitro stimulation (agonists/polarizing cytokines)
studies.publication_title: Blood Milieu in Acute Myocardial Infarction Reprograms Human Macrophages for Trauma Repair
studies.data_availability: Openly available in GEO, reference number GSE172270
studies.author: Lieve Temmerman
studies.orthogonal_validation: Multiparameter high-content functional assays (phagocytosis, lipid uptake, apoptosis, morphology, inflammasome) and multiplex cytokine ELISA on macrophages; immunohistochemistry for von Willebrand Factor in human AMI cardiac tissue
studies.public_datasets_used_for_validation: GSE59867 (PBMC microarray); human infarcted hearts single-cell spatial transcriptomics dataset (Kuppe et al., Nature 2022)

studies.title: circRNA-miRNA-mRNA Deregulated Network in Ischemic Heart Failure Patients
studies.overall_design: Left-ventricle (LV) cardiac tissue was collected from ischemic heart failure (IHF) patients and healthy donor controls. Non-end-stage IHF LV biopsies were obtained during Surgical Ventricular Reconstruction, end-stage IHF LV biopsies during heart transplantation, and healthy LV from age-/sex-matched donor hearts not used for transplant. Total RNA was extracted and profiled by ribominus stranded RNA-Seq (Illumina NovaSeq/NextSeq, paired-end 2×100/2×150). Differential expression of mRNAs was assessed with DESeq2; circRNAs were quantified by CIRIquant/CIRI2 using back-splice junction reads and tested with limma-voom. Dysregulated miRNAs were taken from a prior LV dataset and integrated with circRNA/mRNA predictions to construct a circRNA–miRNA–mRNA ceRNA network, followed by GO/KEGG enrichment; selected interactions (e.g., circBPTF network) were validated experimentally
studies.tissue: Left ventricle myocardium (heart)
studies.sample_collection_method: LV cardiac biopsies from non-end-stage IHF during Surgical Ventricular Reconstruction; LV biopsies from end-stage IHF at transplantation; healthy LV from age- and sex-matched organ donors (hearts not used for transplant)
studies.study_cohorts: - Non-end-stage IHF LV samples and healthy controls for RNA-Seq (20 IHF, 20 controls) - qPCR validation set (12 IHF, 12 controls) - End-stage IHF validation cohort (36 IHF) and healthy controls (44) for circBPTF qPCR
studies.clinical_endpoint: Transcriptome changes and construction/validation of a circRNA–miRNA–mRNA ceRNA network in LV tissue (gene expression changes; pathway enrichment)
studies.comorbidities: Diabetes status considered in prior LV miRNA profiling (diabetic vs non-diabetic IHF); current analysis pooled by IHF vs control
studies.biological_replicates: yes (multiple patient samples per group; independent validation cohorts)
studies.publication_title: circRNA-miRNA-mRNA Deregulated Network in Ischemic Heart Failure Patients
studies.data_availability: Available in GEO (GSE198945); authors request consultation before data release
studies.technology: RNA-Seq; circRNA detection via back-splice junction quantification (CIRIquant/CIRI2)
studies.author: Fabio Martelli (corresponding author)
studies.orthogonal_validation: RT-qPCR validation of circRNAs in LV (12 IHF vs 12 controls); Sanger sequencing of circBPTF back-splice junction; RNase R resistance assay; cellular fractionation; circBPTF/miRNA pull-down assays; siRNA-mediated circBPTF silencing with downstream target (HDAC9, LRRC17) qPCR; cell-cycle analysis by flow cytometry

studies.title: Chamber‑enriched gene expression profiles in failing human hearts with reduced ejection fraction
studies.overall_design: Systematic transcriptomic profiling of all four heart chambers from human donors: bulk RNA-Seq across left atrium (LA), left ventricle (LV), right atrium (RA), and right ventricle (RV) in 10 non‑failing (NF) and 12 HFrEF donors, complemented by single‑nucleus RNA‑Seq (snRNA‑Seq) from LV to deconvolve cell type composition and analyze chamber‑ and disease‑associated pathways and networks. Bulk RNA‑Seq libraries sequenced on Illumina HiSeq; snRNA‑Seq generated with 10x Genomics and sequenced on NovaSeq S4
studies.disease: Heart failure with reduced ejection fraction (HFrEF)
studies.tissue: Primary human cardiac tissue from four chambers: left atrium (LA), left ventricle (LV), right atrium (RA), right ventricle (RV)
studies.sample_collection_method: Postmortem donor heart tissue procurement (non‑transplantable hearts and organ donors) under IRB/ethics approval; warm ischemic time <1 h (AnaBios, IIAM) or postmortem interval within 3 h (Zenas); tissues frozen in liquid N2 and stored at −80°C prior to RNA extraction
studies.study_cohorts: Non‑failing (NF) donors: n=10 (6 male, 4 female), mean age 45, mean LVEF 61.3%, mean BMI 33.1; HFrEF donors: n=12 (7 male, 5 female), mean age 39, mean LVEF 34.9%, mean BMI 25.9. All four chambers sampled per donor
studies.ejection_fraction_info: NF mean LVEF 61.3%; HFrEF mean LVEF 34.9%
studies.total_samples: 84 bulk RNA‑Seq chamber samples (NF: 9 LA, 9 LV, 9 RA, 10 RV; HFrEF: 12 LA, 12 LV, 12 RA, 11 RV)
studies.patient_sample_mapping_available: unclear (patient identity/PHI redacted; per‑donor mapping not provided)
studies.clinical_endpoint: Chamber‑specific gene expression changes, signaling pathway alterations, and estimated cell type composition differences between NF and HFrEF hearts (focus on LA and LV)
studies.biological_replicates: yes (multiple donors per group; multiple chamber samples)
studies.publication_title: Chamber‑enriched gene expression profiles in failing human hearts with reduced ejection fraction
studies.platform_name: Illumina HiSeq platform (bulk RNA‑Seq; GPL11154/HiSeq 2000); 10x Genomics + Illumina NovaSeq S4 for snRNA‑Seq
studies.data_availability: Available in GEO (GSE161472; reference series GSE161473)
studies.author: Xin Luo
studies.public_datasets_used_for_validation: Published heart single‑cell/nucleus datasets from Broad Institute’s Single Cell Portal and Heart Cell Atlas used as deconvolution references

studies.title: Single-cell RNA-seq analysis reveals a crucial role for Collagen Triple Helix Repeat Containing 1 (CTHRC1) cardiac fibroblasts after myocardial infarction
studies.overall_design: Time-course study profiling cardiac fibroblasts after myocardial infarction (MI) in Col1α1-GFP reporter mice using single-cell RNA-seq across healthy, 7, 14 and 30 days post-infarction; complemented with bulk RNA-seq, ATAC-seq, and in vitro functional assays to dissect regulatory pathways. A Cthrc1 knockout mouse model was used to test function. Translational validation included a swine ischemia/reperfusion MI model with zonal biopsies (infarct vs remote) at 8, 60, 180 days and human myocardial biopsies from MI (infarct/remote) and dilated cardiomyopathy patients. Data were deposited to public repositories
studies.tissue: Heart (left ventricle) cardiac tissue and cardiac fibroblasts, sampled from infarct, border, and remote zones; human LV/RV tissue; porcine infarct and remote zones
studies.sample_collection_method: Mouse cardiac fibroblasts isolated by FACS as GFP+/CD31−/CD45− from Col1α1-GFP hearts; zonal dissection of infarct, border, and remote regions for bulk RNA-seq; porcine ischemia/reperfusion model with infarct and remote zone biopsies; human myocardial biopsies from infarct and remote zones
studies.study_cohorts: Mice: healthy and MI at 7, 14, 30 dpi; WT vs Cthrc1 knockout after MI. Swine: infarct and remote zones at 8, 60, 180 dpi. Humans: healthy LV/RV controls; MI patients (infarct and remote zones); dilated cardiomyopathy patients (LV and RV)
studies.ejection_fraction_info: Porcine cohort evaluated at ~6 months (180 dpi) by echocardiography/MRI; animals categorized as preserved (>45%) vs reduced (≤45%) EF; CTHRC1 (and COL1A1) expression in infarct zone negatively correlated with EF
studies.diseased_samples: 30 human cardiac tissue samples (MI LV/RV n=20; DCM LV/RV n=10)
studies.healthy_control_samples: 12 human healthy cardiac tissue samples (healthy LV/RV donors, n=6 donors × 2 regions)
studies.treated_samples: 24 in vitro perturbed fibroblast samples (Runx1 OE n=6; Sox9 OE n=6; TGF-β ± DMSO n=8; TGF-β + LY294002 n=4)
studies.matched_controls: no (healthy human controls are not patient-matched; within-subject IZ/RZ matching exists in pigs)
studies.patient_sample_mapping_available: yes (human MI samples labeled by patient number for LV/RV pairs)
studies.healthy_control_patients: 6 (healthy donors with LV and RV samples)
studies.clinical_endpoint: Ventricular remodeling and cardiac repair outcomes, including survival/mortality after MI, collagen deposition in LV, and cardiac function (EF) and infarct area correlations in pigs
studies.treatment_type: Genetic (Cthrc1 knockout), pharmacological modulation of TGF-β signaling (e.g., PI3K-Akt inhibition), and surgical ischemia/reperfusion in swine model
studies.treatment_agent_name: TGF-β1; LY294002 (PI3K-Akt inhibitor); SB-431542 (ALK5/TGF-βR1 inhibitor); inhibitors targeting SMAD2/3, AKT, p38; ERK inhibitor PD98059 (no effect reported)
studies.biological_replicates: yes (multiple biological replicates per condition and group indicated across experiments)
studies.organism: Mus musculus; Sus scrofa; Homo sapiens
studies.publication_title: Single-cell RNA-seq analysis reveals a crucial role for Collagen Triple Helix Repeat Containing 1 (CTHRC1) cardiac fibroblasts after myocardial infarction
studies.data_availability: All scRNA-seq, bulk RNA-seq, and ATAC-seq data available; deposited to public repositories (GEO GSE132143; SuperSeries referenced as GSE132146)
studies.author: Adrian Ruiz-Villalba
studies.orthogonal_validation: Immunohistochemistry for CTHRC1/DDAH1/FM0D in mouse, pig, and human tissues; wound-healing/migration assays; pharmacological inhibition assays; collagen deposition quantification; survival analysis
studies.validation_using_public_datasets: yes (publicly available ChIP-seq datasets leveraged for TF analyses)
studies.public_datasets_used_for_validation: Publicly available ChIP-seq datasets (specific datasets not listed); JASPAR motif database for TF binding motifs

studies.title: Cytotoxic T-cells drive out come in Inflammatory Dilated Cardiomyopathy
studies.overall_design: Bulk RNA sequencing of human endomyocardial biopsies collected approximately 9 months (IQR 4–26 months) after diagnosis from patients with heart failure due to non-ischemic dilated cardiomyopathy, comparing RNA-seq profiles between patients with high cardiac inflammation and those with low cardiac inflammation
studies.tissue: Endomyocardial biopsy
studies.sample_collection_method: Endomyocardial biopsy
studies.study_cohorts: Two DCM cohorts based on cardiac inflammation: (1) High inflammation (≥7 CD3+ T-cells/mm2 or ≥14 CD45+ leukocytes/mm2) and (2) Low inflammation (<3 CD3+ T-cells/mm2 and <5 CD45+ leukocytes/mm2). Samples labeled as inflammatory (n≈27) and non-inflammatory (n≈10) based on sample titles
studies.clinical_endpoint: Gene expression changes associated with cardiac inflammation status (high vs low) in DCM endomyocardial biopsies
studies.data_availability: Available in GEO
studies.author: Maurits Sikking (Maastricht University)

studies.title: Chromatin accessibility profiling of human coronary arteries identifes disease regulatory variants
studies.overall_design: Bulk genome-wide chromatin accessibility was profiled using ATAC-seq in human coronary artery samples across a range of atherosclerosis disease stages to identify chromatin accessibility QTLs (caQTLs)
studies.tissue: Coronary artery
studies.sample_collection_method: not specified
studies.study_cohorts: Human coronary artery patient samples spanning a range of atherosclerosis/CAD disease stages; no explicit healthy control cohort reported
studies.clinical_endpoint: Identification of chromatin accessibility changes and caQTLs associated with CAD in coronary artery tissue
studies.data_availability: Available in GEO
studies.author: Clint L Miller

studies.title: Integrated multi-omic characterization of congenital heart disease
studies.overall_design: Integrated multi-omic study profiling pediatric congenital heart disease (CHD) using single-nucleus RNA sequencing (snRNA-seq) of ventricular cardiac tissue and single-cell RNA-seq (scRNA-seq) of PBMCs; complemented by imaging mass cytometry (IMC), cardiomyocyte-specific ATAC-seq, immunohistochemistry/IF, RNAscope, and plasma proteome profiling. Cardiac snRNA-seq generated 157,293 nuclei from CHD and control hearts; PBMC scRNA-seq combined CHD with public controls. Sequencing performed on 10x Genomics Single Cell 3' v3 libraries and sequenced on Illumina instruments
studies.disease: Congenital heart disease (including hypoplastic left heart syndrome (HLHS; neonatal and failing), Tetralogy of Fallot (TOF)) and cardiomyopathies (dilated cardiomyopathy (DCM), hypertrophic cardiomyopathy (HCM)); includes failing HLHS (heart failure)
studies.tissue: Cardiac ventricular tissue (right ventricle for HLHS/TOF; left ventricle for DCM, HCM, and failing HLHS) and peripheral blood mononuclear cells (PBMCs)
studies.sample_collection_method: Cardiac tissues and blood collected during cardiothoracic surgeries at Texas Children’s Hospital; control donor hearts procured from organ donors at University of Kentucky and Washington University; blood drawn before bypass into EDTA vacutainers; tissues flash-frozen or fixed for histology
studies.study_cohorts: Paediatric cohorts included: nine CHD heart samples (TOF, neonatal HLHS, failing HLHS with acute heart failure, DCM, HCM) and four control paediatric donor hearts; PBMCs from CHD patients and publicly available control PBMCs. HLHS and TOF samples from right ventricle; DCM, HCM, failing HLHS from left ventricle
studies.total_samples: 43 GSM libraries across PBMC scRNA-seq and cardiac snRNA-seq
studies.diseased_samples: 30 (12 PBMC scRNA-seq patient samples and 18 cardiac snRNA-seq patient libraries)
studies.healthy_control_samples: 13 (1 PBMC donor sample and 12 cardiac donor libraries)
studies.patient_sample_mapping_available: yes (sample titles carry patient/donor identifiers such as P1, P8, UK1, WU198LV/RV)
studies.total_diseased_patients: 17 unique patients across PBMC and cardiac datasets (e.g., P1–P4, P8, P25–P28, P32, P33, P36–P41, P64, P75, P86)
studies.healthy_control_patients: 5 unique controls (PBMC Donor1; cardiac donor hearts UK1, UK2, WU198 (LV/RV from same donor), WU13235)
studies.clinical_endpoint: Characterization of cardiac cell states and gene-expression programs, intercellular signalling networks, and systemic immune features associated with CHD progression
studies.biological_replicates: yes (multiple biological patients per diagnosis and technical replicates per library)
studies.publication_title: Integrated multi-omic characterization of congenital heart disease
studies.data_availability: Available in GEO (GSE203274). The publication also reports deposition at GEO GSE203275 and availability via Broad Single Cell Portal SCP1852
studies.technology: Single-nucleus RNA-seq (cardiac tissue) and single-cell RNA-seq (PBMCs) using 10x Genomics Single Cell 3' v3
studies.author: Matthew C. Hill
studies.orthogonal_validation: Imaging mass cytometry to define perivascular microenvironment; RNAscope RNA FISH for CRIM1/CORIN and THBS1; immunofluorescence/immunohistochemistry for YAP, MYC, PTX3; cardiomyocyte-specific ATAC-seq; plasma cytokine proteome profiling; histology (H&E, Masson’s trichrome)
studies.public_datasets_used_for_validation: Control heart maturation datasets from Sim et al., 2021; public PBMC datasets from Caron et al., 2020 and Zheng et al., 2017

studies.title: Association of Circulating, Inflammatory-Response Exosomal mRNAs With Acute Myocardial Infarction
studies.overall_design: Case-control study of 10 AMI patients, 8 stable CAD patients, and 10 healthy individuals. Venous blood was collected at admission before coronary angiography into EDTA tubes; plasma was separated, and exosomes were isolated. Exosomal total RNA was extracted from 1 mL plasma using the exoRNeasy Serum/Plasma kit. SMART technology was used for library construction, and sequencing was performed on an Illumina NovaSeq 6000. Reads were aligned with HISAT2, mRNAs/lncRNAs annotated with GENCODE, and circRNAs identified with ACFS. Exosomes were characterized by TEM, NanoSight particle sizing, and Western blot for CD9/CD63. Immune cell fractions were inferred using CIBERSORT. Differential expression analysis was performed via NetworkAnalyst using DESeq2; GO enrichment was conducted with clusterProfiler; co-expression modules were identified using WGCNA; and diagnostic performance was assessed via ROC analysis
studies.disease: Acute Myocardial Infarction (with stable coronary artery disease and healthy controls for comparison)
studies.tissue: Plasma exosomes (exosome from plasma)
studies.sample_collection_method: Venous blood draw into EDTA tubes at admission before coronary angiography; plasma separated; exosomal RNA extracted from 1 mL plasma
studies.study_cohorts: 10 AMI patients (9 STEMI, 1 NSTEMI), 8 stable CAD patients, and 10 healthy individuals
studies.clinical_endpoint: Exosomal long RNA gene expression profiles and identification of exosomal mRNA biomarkers (ALPL, CXCR2) associated with neutrophil-mediated acute inflammatory response in AMI; diagnostic performance assessed by ROC
studies.publication_title: Association of Circulating, Inflammatory-Response Exosomal mRNAs With Acute Myocardial Infarction
studies.data_availability: Available in GEO (GSE159657)
studies.technology: RNA-Seq of plasma exosomal long RNAs
studies.author: Guo-dong He
studies.orthogonal_validation: Exosome characterization by TEM, nanoparticle tracking (NanoSight), and Western blot for exosomal markers CD9 and CD63

studies.title: Transcriptome and proteome profiling of activated cardiac fibroblasts supports target prioritization in cardiac fibrosis
studies.overall_design: Comparative multi-omics study profiling quiescent versus TGF-β1–activated human cardiac fibroblasts from three sources (adult primary CF, fetal primary CF, and hiPSC-derived CF). Cells were maintained quiescent and then activated with TGF-β1; RNA-seq (QuantSeq 3' mRNA-Seq; Illumina NextSeq 500) and quantitative proteomics (SWATH-MS) were performed with three independent biological replicates per condition and cell source; analyses emphasized extracellular matrix and membrane remodeling and included target prioritization and limited pharmacological validation
studies.tissue: Human cardiac fibroblasts: adult primary CF (from adult heart), fetal primary CF (from fetal heart), and hiPSC-derived cardiac fibroblasts
studies.sample_collection_method: In vitro cell harvest: cells detached with Accutase for RNA extraction and with Versene for proteomics prior to lysis
studies.study_cohorts: For each CF source (aHCF, fHCF, hiPSC-CF): control (quiescent; SB431542) vs TGF-β1–treated (activated) for 48 h; three independent experiments per source. MK-0429 was added after TGF-β1 in a validation assay
studies.clinical_endpoint: Molecular remodeling (gene expression and protein abundance) upon cardiac fibroblast activation; identification of candidate antifibrotic targets
studies.treatment_type: pharmacological/biological (growth factor stimulation and small-molecule inhibitor)
studies.publication_title: Transcriptome and proteome profiling of activated cardiac fibroblasts supports target prioritization in cardiac fibrosis
studies.data_availability: Available in GEO (GSE202714) and PRIDE (PXD034244)
studies.author: Daniel Simão
studies.orthogonal_validation: Immunofluorescence for α-SMA stress fibers and collagen I deposition; qRT-PCR for marker genes; ELISA for pro-collagen Iα1 secretion

studies.title: Single-nucleus chromatin accessibility profiling highlights regulatory mechanisms of coronary artery disease risk
studies.overall_design: Single-nucleus ATAC-seq (10x Genomics) was performed on frozen human coronary artery segments (LAD, LCX, RCA) from 41 patients with varying atherosclerosis stages. A total of 44 coronary segments were processed, yielding 28,316 high-quality nuclei and 14 cell clusters. Analyses included peak calling (~323k peaks), cell type annotation via gene activity scores and scRNA-seq integration, motif enrichment, LD score regression, Peak2Gene co-accessibility, chromatin accessibility QTL (caQTL) mapping with RASQUAL, and machine learning-based variant effect prediction. Library prep used 10x Genomics Chromium; sequencing was on Illumina NovaSeq 6000. Samples were procured from explanted transplant hearts and non-diseased donor hearts, dissected and snap frozen
studies.tissue: Human coronary artery segments (LAD, LCX, RCA)
studies.sample_collection_method: Surgical procurement of coronary segments from explanted transplant recipient hearts and from non-diseased donor hearts rejected for transplant; dissection of proximal coronary vessels on ice followed by snap-freezing
studies.study_cohorts: 41 patients spanning healthy/non-lesion controls and atherosclerotic lesions (subclinical atheroma and advanced fibrocalcific plaques); sources included explanted hearts (ischemic/non-ischemic cardiomyopathy) and non-diseased donor hearts rejected for transplant; lesion stages annotated by Stary categories I–VI
studies.total_samples: 44 frozen coronary artery segments/snATAC libraries from 41 patients; 28,316 nuclei profiled
studies.clinical_endpoint: Cell type–resolved chromatin accessibility and cis-regulatory programs in coronary artery across CAD stages; prioritization of functional CAD GWAS variants and target genes via caQTLs, co-accessibility, and predictive modeling
studies.comorbidities: Explanted hearts classified as ischemic or non-ischemic cardiomyopathy; prior ischemic events documented
studies.publication_title: Single-nucleus chromatin accessibility profiling highlights regulatory mechanisms of coronary artery disease risk
studies.platform_name: 10x Genomics Chromium Single Cell ATAC; Illumina NovaSeq 6000
studies.data_availability: Raw and processed snATAC-seq data available in GEO (GSE175621, GSE188422); genotypes in dbGaP (phs002855.v1.p1)
studies.author: Clint L. Miller
studies.orthogonal_validation: Immunofluorescence staining for PRDM16 and alpha-smooth muscle actin with histological assessment (H&E, Movat pentachrome) across lesion stages; corroboration with GTEx arterial eQTLs
studies.public_datasets_used_for_validation: Human coronary artery scRNA-seq from Wirka et al. (GSE131780); GTEx v8 artery tissues; CAD GWAS (van der Harst 2018; Koyama 2020); STARNET networks; UK Biobank/MVP GWAS summaries

studies.title: RNAseq Profiling of Blood from Patients with Coronary Artery Disease: Signature of a T Cell Imbalance
studies.overall_design: Prospective observational RNA-seq study of whole blood from 177 patients undergoing elective invasive coronary angiography (ICA). Patients were categorized using stress testing and ICA into four groups: Control (Stress−/CAD−), INOCA (Stress+/CAD−), CAD+/Stress−, and CAD+/Stress+; blood was drawn prior to ICA into Tempus RNA tubes by peripheral venipuncture or an indwelling catheter. Total RNA was DNase-treated, rRNA-depleted (Illumina TruSeq Stranded Total RNA, Ribo-Zero), sequenced on Illumina NextSeq 500 (2×75 bp). Reads were trimmed and aligned to hg38 with STAR; gene-level counts were obtained and DEGs identified using DESeq2. Cell-type associations were evaluated using Human Protein Atlas; additional analyses included hierarchical clustering and WGCNA
studies.disease: Coronary artery disease (CAD) and ischemia with non-obstructive coronary arteries (INOCA)
studies.tissue: Whole blood
studies.sample_collection_method: Peripheral venipuncture or indwelling catheter blood draw into Tempus RNA stabilizing tubes prior to ICA
studies.study_cohorts: Four cohorts defined by stress testing and ICA: Control (Stress−/CAD−, n=40), INOCA (Stress+/CAD−, n=39), CAD+/Stress− (n=38), CAD+/Stress+ (n=60); total n=177
studies.ejection_fraction_info: Ejection fraction (mean ± SD): Control 55.9±10.5%, INOCA 55.5±10.7%, CAD+/Stress− 60.2±8.52% (p<0.05), CAD+/Stress+ 55.7±11.5%; overall 56.7±10.6%

studies.author: Timothy A. McCaffrey

studies.title: Targeting immune–fibroblast cell communication in heart failure
studies.overall_design: Multiomic, single-cell study of human left-ventricle tissue integrating CITE-seq (RNA + surface protein) and 10x Multiome (paired single-nucleus RNA-seq and ATAC-seq), plus spatial transcriptomics, to map immune–fibroblast interactions across healthy donors, acute MI, and chronic heart failure (ICM, NICM). Human hearts (n=45 total across conditions) were profiled, including fresh cells from acutely infarcted regions; CITE-seq included 22 patients (6 donors, 4 AMI, 6 ICM, 6 NICM; 143,804 cells), and Multiome comprised 23 samples across donors, AMI, ICM, NICM. In vivo mouse models (Ang II/PE infusion; TAC; LAD ligation) and interventions (fibroblast IL-1R deletion; CCR2+ monocyte/macrophage IL-1β deletion; anti–IL‑1β mAb) were used to test causality, alongside imaging (68Ga‑FAPi PET), histology, RNAscope, IF, qPCR, flow cytometry, and echocardiography. Raw/processed data are deposited to GEO
studies.disease: Acute myocardial infarction and heart failure (ischaemic and non‑ischaemic cardiomyopathies)
studies.tissue: Human cardiac left ventricle tissue (apex in donors/ICM/NICM; infarct region in AMI)
studies.sample_collection_method: Fresh left-ventricle tissue obtained from explanted hearts at transplantation, LVAD cores, or donation after circulatory death; infarct region dissected in AMI; tissues enzymatically dissociated for CITE-seq or nuclei isolated for Multiome; live cells/nuclei sorted by FACS prior to library prep
studies.study_cohorts: Human cohorts: healthy donors; AMI (<3 months post-MI); ICM (>3 months post-MI from CAD); NICM (idiopathic dilated cardiomyopathy). CITE-seq: 6 donors, 4 AMI, 6 ICM, 6 NICM. Inclusion criteria specified per group

studies.diseased_samples: CITE-seq diseased patients: 16 (4 AMI, 6 ICM, 6 NICM)

studies.title: Multi-ancestry genetic analysis of gene regulation in coronary artery prioritizes disease risk loci
studies.overall_design: Bulk RNA-sequencing of human coronary artery segments collected from transplant recipients and non-diseased donor hearts, with dissection of proximal 5–6 cm of major coronary arteries, snap-freezing, and RNA-seq library preparation/sequencing. Samples span lesion-free/subclinical and advanced/complex atherosclerotic lesions. RNA-seq data processed with standard pipelines; data used to compare lesion vs non-lesion expression and, in the broader study context, to map ASE, eQTL, and sQTL across ancestries in coronary arteries
studies.tissue: Coronary artery
studies.sample_collection_method: Surgical procurement of explanted human hearts and non-diseased donor hearts; dissection of proximal 5–6 cm segments of LAD/LCX/RCA coronary arteries; tissues rinsed and snap-frozen in liquid nitrogen
studies.study_cohorts: Human coronary artery segments categorized as non-lesion (lesion-free or subclinical/early) vs lesion (advanced/complex atherosclerotic lesions; stage IV–V Stary classification). Normal arteries from donor hearts with LVEF > 50%
studies.ejection_fraction_info: All normal arteries originated from hearts with LVEF greater than 50%
studies.clinical_endpoint: Gene expression changes in coronary artery tissue across lesion status (lesion vs non-lesion), including evaluation of markers associated with plaque stability/progression
studies.publication_title: Integrative single-cell meta-analysis reveals disease-relevant vascular cell states and markers in human atherosclerosis
studies.data_availability: All raw and processed bulk RNA-sequencing data are publicly available in GEO under accession GSE225650

studies.title: Whole blood transcriptome profile at hospital admission discriminates between patients with ST-segment elevation and non-ST-segment elevation acute myocardial infarction
studies.overall_design: Case-control study enrolling 120 consecutive AMI patients (60 STEMI, 58 NSTEMI). Peripheral whole blood was drawn on hospital admission before any intervention. Discovery RNA-Seq compared 15 STEMI vs 15 NSTEMI matched for age, sex, and cardiovascular risk factors; remaining unmatched patients were used for RT-qPCR validation (STEMI n=45, NSTEMI n=43; two NSTEMI excluded for sample degradation). RNA was poly(A)+, DNase-treated and globin-depleted; libraries sequenced using SOLiD paired-end; reads mapped to HG38 with TopHat/Bowtie; transcript assembly with Cufflinks. Differential expression used edgeR with RUVSeq correction; functional enrichment via GSEA; associations with peak cTnI modeled by linear regression. The GEO dataset comprises the 30 discovery RNA-Seq samples (15 STEMI, 15 NSTEMI)
studies.tissue: Whole blood (peripheral blood)
studies.sample_collection_method: Peripheral blood draw from an antecubital vein into Tempus Blood RNA tubes at admission, before any medical intervention
studies.study_cohorts: Discovery RNA-Seq cohort: 15 STEMI vs 15 NSTEMI matched for age, sex, and cardiovascular risk factors; Validation cohort: 45 STEMI vs 43 NSTEMI (2 NSTEMI excluded for sample inadequacy) analyzed by RT-qPCR
studies.ejection_fraction_info: LVEF in study cohort: NSTEMI median 57.5% (Q1–Q3 50.5–63.8) vs STEMI 52.0% (47.5–58.2), P=0.19; Validation cohort: NSTEMI 54.5% (48.0–61.3) vs STEMI 50.0% (40.0–54.8), P=0.02
studies.clinical_endpoint: Differential blood gene expression distinguishing STEMI vs NSTEMI and prediction of infarct size indexed by peak cardiac troponin I (cTnI) from admission transcript levels
studies.comorbidities: Hypertension, hypercholesterolemia, diabetes; prior AMI; prior PCI; prior CABG; excluded major comorbidities such as severe chronic kidney disease, chronic atrial fibrillation, prior stroke, malignancy, infections, autoimmune diseases
studies.treatment_type: Interventional cardiology/surgical procedures during hospitalization (PCI, CABG); samples collected before intervention
studies.treatment_agent_name: Primary PCI; early (<24 h) PCI; non-urgent PCI; elective CABG
studies.publication_title: Whole blood transcriptome profile at hospital admission discriminates between patients with ST-segment elevation and non-ST-segment elevation acute myocardial infarction
studies.data_availability: Available in GEO under accession GSE103182
studies.author: Gualtiero I. Colombo
studies.orthogonal_validation: RT-qPCR technical validation in discovery cohort and biological validation in an independent unmatched cohort (8 genes), with strong correlation between RNA-Seq and qPCR; normalization to AP2A2 and EIF3F; three technical replicates per sample

studies.title: Cardiac DNA Methylation Underlies Racial and Socioeconomic Disparities among Patients with End-Stage Heart Failure (RNA-seq)
studies.overall_design: Pilot analysis of myocardial genome-wide DNA methylation using Illumina HumanMethylation450 with tandem RNA-sequencing in 11 NYHA Stage IV heart failure subjects, followed by confirmatory analysis of 31 human heart failure biopsies using the Illumina MethylationEPIC array. Unsupervised analyses identified race as the principal determinant of variability; RNA-seq was performed from left ventricular tissue samples

studies.sample_collection_method: Myocardial biopsy (left ventricle)
studies.study_cohorts: End-stage (NYHA IV) heart failure patients grouped by race/ethnicity: African American, Caucasian American, Asian American, and Eastern African
studies.clinical_endpoint: Race-associated differences in cardiac DNA methylation and transcriptome in failing human hearts
studies.publication_title: Racial and socioeconomic disparity associates with differences in cardiac DNA methylation among men with end-stage heart failure
studies.data_availability: Available in GEO
studies.author: Mark E Pepin

studies.title: In vivo generation of post-infarct human cardiac muscle by laminin-promoted cardiovascular progenitors [H1 differentiation protocol]
studies.overall_design: Human embryonic stem cells (H1) were cultured on laminin substrates (LN-521 and LN-221) in RPMI/B27 minus insulin. Differentiation was induced at confluency with CHIR99021 on day 0 and IWP2 on day 3, followed by continued culture up to day 90 with scheduled media changes. Bulk RNA was extracted from replicate wells at multiple time points (days 0, 1, 3, 5, 7, 9, 11, 14, 20, 30, 90) to profile gene expression dynamics across differentiation
studies.tissue: Human embryonic stem cells (H1) and derived cardiovascular progenitors (in vitro)
studies.sample_collection_method: In vitro cell culture; RNA extracted from replicate wells at specified time points during differentiation on LN-521/LN-221 substrates
studies.study_cohorts: Time-course cohorts by differentiation day with replicates: Pluripotent H1 (n=3); Day 0 (n=3); Day 1 (n=3); Day 3 (n=3); Day 5 (n=5); Day 7 (n=5); Day 9 (n=5); Day 11 (n=5); Day 14 (n=3); Day 20 (n=3); Day 30 (n=3); Day 90 (n=3)
studies.clinical_endpoint: Gene expression changes across hESC-to-cardiovascular progenitor differentiation; additionally, post-transplant heart function by echocardiography and histological identification of human heart muscle bundles in myocardial infarction mice
studies.treatment_type: Cell culture differentiation using extracellular matrix (laminins) and small-molecule pathway modulators
studies.treatment_agent_name: Laminin-221 (LN-221), Laminin-521 (LN-521), CHIR99021 (GSK3 inhibitor), IWP2 (Wnt production inhibitor)
studies.biological_replicates: yes; triplicate wells for most time points, and 5 wells for days 5, 7, and 9
studies.data_availability: Available in GEO
studies.author: Aida Moreno-Moral
studies.orthogonal_validation: Single-cell RNA sequencing of CVPs; echocardiography and histological analysis after transplantation into myocardial infarction mice

studies.title: Novel Long Noncoding RNA HEAT4 Affects Monocyte Subtypes, Reducing Inflammation and Promoting Vascular Healing
studies.overall_design: Single-cell RNA sequencing (scRNA-seq) was performed on PBMCs from heart failure (HF) patients (n=8) and controls (n=3) to discover and characterize the human-specific lncRNA HEAT4 and its role in monocyte subsets. Additional cohorts included bulk RNA-seq of PBMCs (HF vs controls), qPCR validation in whole blood and PBMCs across HF, acute myocardial infarction (AMI; STEMI/NSTEMI), and cardiogenic shock, cytokine stimulation of primary human monocytes (IL-10, TNFα, IL-1β, IL-6), gain- and loss-of-function assays, RNA–protein interaction assays (antisense pulldown, RIP), ChIP-seq for S100A9, endothelial barrier assays, and a humanized mouse carotid injury model with injection of human monocytes overexpressing HEAT4
studies.disease: Heart failure; additional cohorts include acute myocardial infarction and cardiogenic shock
studies.tissue: Peripheral blood mononuclear cells (PBMCs); whole blood used in validation cohorts
studies.sample_collection_method: Blood draw with PBMC isolation from controls and HF patients; whole blood draws for qPCR cohorts
studies.study_cohorts: Cohort 1: PBMC scRNA-seq from controls (n=3) and HF patients (n=8) . Cohort 2: Bulk RNA-seq of PBMCs from 4 patients with ischemic cardiomyopathy and 4 controls . Cohort 3: qPCR on whole blood from 63 HF patients and 38 controls with >7 years follow-up . Cohort 4: qPCR on PBMCs from 43 AMI patients and 23 controls (AMI cohort referenced in [32]) . Cohort 5: qPCR on whole blood from 4 cardiogenic shock patients (SHOCK-COOL trial) and 5 controls . Humanized mouse carotid injury model with injection of human CD14+ monocytes ± HEAT4 overexpression
studies.ejection_fraction_info: HF with reduced ejection fraction defined as LVEF ≤40% of ischemic origin
studies.clinical_endpoint: Primary molecular endpoints: gene expression changes and monocyte subtype composition in PBMCs; clinical endpoint: prediction of all-cause mortality over >7 years in HF cohort 3; functional endpoints include endothelial barrier recovery and re-endothelialization after carotid injury in mice
studies.treatment_type: Pharmacological (cytokine stimulation of monocytes in vitro); surgical/procedural (carotid artery injury model in mice); clinical procedural (mild hypothermia in cardiogenic shock trial cohort)
studies.treatment_agent_name: Interleukin-10 (IL-10), tumor necrosis factor alpha (TNFα), IL-1β, IL-6; mild hypothermia (SHOCK-COOL)
studies.organism: Homo sapiens; Mus musculus (NOD.CB17-Prkdc scid/Rj for in vivo vascular injury model)
studies.publication_title: Novel Long Noncoding RNA HEAT4 Affects Monocyte Subtypes, Reducing Inflammation and Promoting Vascular Healing
studies.data_availability: Available in GEO (GSE267646 per article); mass spectrometry proteomics in PRIDE (PXD052076)
studies.author: Jasmin M. Kneuer; corresponding author: Jes-Niels Boeckel
studies.orthogonal_validation: qPCR validation in independent cohorts (whole blood and PBMCs) ; ELISA for cytokines and S100A8/A9 ; RNA–protein pulldown and RIP ; ChIP-seq for S100A9 ; flow cytometry for monocyte subsets ; endothelial barrier assays and mouse carotid re-endothelialization

studies.title: In vivo generation of post-infarct human cardiac muscle by laminin-promoted cardiovascular progenitors [HS1001 differentiation protocol]
studies.overall_design: Human embryonic stem cells (HS1001) were cultured on LN-521 and LN-221 in chemically defined, xeno-free conditions. Differentiation was initiated at confluency with CHIR99021 (GSK3 inhibitor) on day 0, followed by IWP2 (Wnt production inhibitor) on day 3. Cells were maintained in RPMI+B27 minus insulin for 10 days, then switched to RPMI+B27 CTS. Bulk RNA was collected across a time course with triplicate wells at days 0, 1, 3, 11, 14, 20, 30, and 90, and five wells at days 5, 7, and 9 for RNA-Seq. Single-cell RNA-seq was also performed on derived CVPs to assess subpopulations and reproducibility
studies.tissue: Human embryonic stem cells / cardiovascular progenitors (in vitro differentiated)
studies.sample_collection_method: In vitro culture and differentiation of hESCs followed by RNA extraction from cultured wells at specified time points
studies.study_cohorts: HS1001 hESCs sampled across differentiation time points: pluripotent (pluri), D0, D1, D3, D5, D7, D9, D11, D14, D20, D30, D90; typically 3 biological replicates per time point and 5 replicates at D5, D7, D9. CVPs also assessed by scRNA-seq; CVPs transplanted into MI mice for functional assessment
studies.clinical_endpoint: Gene expression dynamics during hESC-to-cardiovascular progenitor differentiation; in vivo cardiac function by echocardiography and histological identification of human muscle bundle formation after CVP transplantation into MI mice
studies.treatment_type: Pharmacological (small-molecule Wnt/GSK3 modulation), extracellular matrix/biomaterial-assisted differentiation (laminin coatings), and cell therapy transplantation in MI mouse model
studies.data_availability: Available in GEO
studies.author: Aida Moreno-Moral
studies.orthogonal_validation: Echocardiography to assess heart function in MI mice and histological identification of human heart muscle bundle formation post-transplantation

studies.title: Whole-Blood Transcriptome Unveils Altered Immune Response in Acute Myocardial Infarction Patients With Aortic Valve Sclerosis
studies.overall_design: Cross-sectional observational study enrolling 110 AMI patients (STEMI or NSTEMI) at a single center (2012–2015); AVSc status assessed by echocardiography. Peripheral venous whole blood was drawn at hospital admission before any medical intervention and stabilized in Tempus tubes. Globin-depleted, poly(A)+ RNA was prepared for RNA-seq using SOLiD (Applied Biosystems), paired-end 75/35 bp; reads aligned to GRCh38 with STAR and Bowtie2 and quantified with featureCounts. Analytical workflow included feature selection (GARS), classification (DaMiRseq), DE analysis with DESeq2 adjusted for latent variables, age, and prior events/interventions, followed by GSEA and immune cell-type enrichment. Cardiovascular events were collected over long-term follow-up and Cox models estimated hazard associated with AVSc
studies.disease: Acute myocardial infarction; Aortic valve sclerosis
studies.tissue: Whole blood
studies.sample_collection_method: Peripheral venous blood draw at hospital admission before any medical intervention; collected into Tempus Blood RNA tubes
studies.study_cohorts: AMI patients with aortic valve sclerosis (AVSc; n=44) vs AMI patients without AVSc (no-AVSc; n=66); both STEMI and NSTEMI included
studies.clinical_endpoint: Adverse cardiovascular events during follow-up (rehospitalization for acute heart failure or AMI, unplanned coronary revascularization, cerebrovascular events, and cardiovascular mortality) over up to 10 years; hazard ratios comparing AVSc vs no-AVSc
studies.comorbidities: Hypertension; hypercholesterolemia; diabetes; current smoking; previous AMI; previous PCI; previous CABG
studies.publication_title: Whole-Blood Transcriptome Unveils Altered Immune Response in Acute Myocardial Infarction Patients With Aortic Valve Sclerosis
studies.platform_name: GPL16288 (AB 5500xl Genetic Analyzer); SOLiD Sequencing by Oligonucleotide Ligation and Detection (Applied Biosystems)
studies.data_availability: Anonymized RNA-seq data publicly available in GEO (GSE218474); anonymized metadata and raw data in Zenodo (via request to corresponding author); analysis code on GitHub

studies.title: Myocardial Cytoskeletal Adaptations in Advanced Kidney Disease
studies.overall_design: Three-arm observational cross-sectional controlled cohort study using explanted human hearts from deceased donors: hemodialysis-dependent CKD (HD), hypertension with preserved renal function (HTN), and healthy controls. Left ventricular (LV) free wall tissue underwent gross pathology, histology, and next-generation RNA sequencing; mechanistic in vitro studies used primary human ventricular cardiac fibroblasts exposed to uremic serum, inflammatory cytokine (TNF-α), and mineral stressors (phosphate/calcium), plus FAK siRNA knockdown; RNA-seq data deposited in GEO (GSE160145)

studies.tissue: Left ventricular free wall (human heart); primary human ventricular cardiac fibroblasts (in vitro)

studies.study_cohorts: Hemodialysis-dependent CKD (n=19); Hypertension with preserved renal function (n=10); Healthy controls (n=21); peritoneal dialysis excluded
studies.clinical_endpoint: Myocardial cytoskeletal adaptations/dysregulation (focal adhesion pathway), myocardial fibrosis, mitochondrial bioenergetics dysfunction, and LV gene expression changes
studies.comorbidities: Diabetes; hypertension; coronary artery disease; prior cardiovascular disease
studies.treatment_type: In vitro treatments: pro-inflammatory cytokine and mineral stressors; pooled uremic serum; RNA interference (siRNA knockdown of FAK)

studies.title: Proteomic Comparison of Acute Myocardial Infarction and Stress Cardiomyopathy in Women
studies.overall_design: Prospective case–control proteomics study of serum from normal controls and women with acute AMI or acute SCM. Non-fasting venous blood was drawn after cardiac catheterization but within 24 h of presentation, collected in SST tubes, processed, and stored at −80°C. Acute samples: AMI (n=12), SCM (n=15), controls (n=6). Two-week follow-up samples: AMI (n=4), SCM (n=11). Relative concentrations of 1,310 predetermined serum proteins were quantified per sample using the SOMAscan aptamer-based assay
studies.disease: Acute myocardial infarction (AMI) and stress cardiomyopathy (SCM)
studies.tissue: Serum
studies.sample_collection_method: Non-fasting venous blood draw into SST tubes after cardiac catheterization, within 24 h of presentation

studies.ejection_fraction_info: Acute phase LVEF was similar between AMI and SCM (39 ± 12% vs. 37 ± 12%, p=0.479). At 2-week follow-up, SCM showed greater recovery (LVEF 60 ± 7% vs. 45 ± 13%, p<0.001)
studies.clinical_endpoint: Circulating serum proteome differences (differentially expressed proteins and pathway enrichment) between AMI and SCM vs. normal; echocardiographic cardiac function (LVEF) acutely and at 2 weeks
studies.comorbidities: Hypertension; Diabetes mellitus; Depression/anxiety; Smoking history

studies.title: Single-Cell RNA Sequencing of Peripheral Blood Mononuclear Cells From Acute Myocardial Infarction
studies.overall_design: Prospective study of AMI patients undergoing PCI at Shanghai Tongji Hospital. Plaque phenotype (plaque rupture, PR, vs non-plaque rupture/erosion, NPR) was determined by intravascular optical coherence tomography (OCT). Peripheral arterial blood was collected within 6 hours of symptom onset before angiography; samples were drawn into EDTA tubes prior to heparin or contrast. PBMCs were isolated using Ficoll, processed within 2 hours, and subjected to 10x Genomics Chromium Next GEM Single Cell 3' v3 library preparation and sequenced on Illumina NovaSeq. Data were processed with Cell Ranger (GRCh38), analyzed with Seurat for clustering, differential expression, Monocle2 for pseudotime, SCENIC for TF activity, and CellChat for ligand–receptor communication analysis. Ten AMI patients (5 PR, 5 NPR) were included for scRNA-seq
studies.tissue: Peripheral blood mononuclear cells (PBMCs)
studies.sample_collection_method: Peripheral arterial blood draw before coronary angiography (within 6 hours of symptom onset), collected in EDTA tubes; PBMCs isolated by Ficoll density gradient
studies.study_cohorts: Ten AMI patients profiled: 5 with plaque rupture (PR) and 5 without plaque rupture (NPR/erosion) as identified by OCT; AMI classified as STEMI or NSTEMI per definitions
studies.ejection_fraction_info: Exclusion criterion: left ventricular ejection fraction <30%
studies.clinical_endpoint: Single-cell gene expression profiles and immune cell subset differences in PBMCs associated with plaque rupture versus erosion, including differential expression and inferred cell–cell communication

studies.data_availability: Available in GEO, GSE269269

studies.overall_design: Single-nucleus RNA sequencing (snRNA-seq) of apical left ventricle heart tissue from three groups: fetal (19–20 weeks), pediatric nondiseased (ND; 4–14 years), and early-onset pediatric DCM (5–10 years). studies.title: Implication of Sphingolipid Metabolism Gene Dysregulation and Cardiac Sphingosine-1-Phosphate Accumulation in Heart Failure
studies.overall_design: Human left ventricle myocardial tissue was obtained from explanted hearts of heart failure (HF) patients undergoing transplantation and non-diseased donor controls. mRNA-seq (HF n=26; control n=10) and ncRNA-seq (HF n=42; control n=8) were performed to profile sphingolipid metabolism genes and regulatory miRNAs. Tissue sphingolipids (S1P, ceramide, sphingosine, sphingomyelin) were quantified by ELISA in LV homogenates (HF n=36; control n=5). Associations between molecular measures and ventricular remodeling parameters (LV mass, LVESD, LVEDD) were analyzed. Tissue samples were consistently taken near the LV apex and processed under standardized conditions

studies.title: Long non-coding and coding RNA profiling using strand-specific RNA-seq in human hypertrophic cardiomyopathy
studies.overall_design: Strand-specific RNA-seq was performed on myocardial tissues from 28 HCM patients and 9 healthy donors. HCM patients were undergoing septal myectomy; myocardial tissues were collected during surgery and snap-frozen, while normal samples were obtained from donor hearts abandoned during cardiac transplant. rRNA was removed, strand-specific libraries were prepared (NEBNext Ultra Directional), and sequencing was done on the Illumina HiSeq X Ten (2×150 bp). Quantification used kallisto with 100 bootstraps, and differential expression between HCM and normal samples was analyzed with sleuth, following a defined bioinformatics workflow

studies.title: Single-cell transcriptomic profiling provides insights into disease-related processes in human hypertrophic cardiomyopathy
studies.overall_design: Single-cell RNA sequencing (scRNA-seq) was performed on septal myectomy tissue from patients with hypertrophic cardiomyopathy (HCM) using SORT-seq/CEL-seq2; a total of 2,292 cells from five septal myectomy samples were analyzed. Public scRNA-seq datasets of healthy adult human cardiomyocytes (Ctrl1 LV CMs, n=1,400; Ctrl2 septal CMs, n=27,604) were integrated to compare HCM versus healthy cells while minimizing batch effects. Index-sorting data (FSC-A) were collected for two HCM patients to link cell size to gene expression and identify hypertrophy-associated genes. Additional validation included RT-PCR on 97 HCM myectomy RNAs and 9 non-failing donor hearts, histology/immunohistochemistry (ANP), and immunofluorescence (MLC-2) with WGA staining to assess cell size distribution. Libraries were sequenced on Illumina NextSeq 500 and mapped with a STAR/featureCounts pipeline

studies.sample_collection_method: Surgical septal myectomy to relieve left ventricular outflow tract obstruction
studies.study_cohorts: - HCM cohort: 5 patients for scRNA-seq (2 with index sorting); 2,292 single cells included. - Healthy cohorts: public scRNA-seq of adult human cardiomyocytes from Wang et al. (Ctrl1, n=1,400 LV CMs) and Litvinukova et al. (Ctrl2, n=27,604 septal CMs). - Validation cohort: 97 additional HCM myectomy RNA samples and 9 non-failing donor hearts for RT-PCR

studies.diseased_samples: 2,292 single cardiomyocytes from 5 HCM myectomy samples

studies.public_datasets_used_for_validation: Wang et al., 2020 (GSE121893; GSE109816); Litvinukova et al., 2020 (ERP123138)

studies.title: Comparative RNA-sequencing analysis of myocardial and circulating small RNAs in human heart failure and their utility as biomarkers
studies.overall_design: Human study profiling small RNAs by sequencing in left ventricular myocardium and in circulation (plasma and serum) across heart failure stages. Myocardial RNA was isolated from left ventricular tissue of advanced HF patients due to dilated or ischemic cardiomyopathy at LVAD implantation and at LVAD explantation during heart transplantation, as well as from non-failing adult and fetal hearts. Circulating RNA (EDTA plasma and serum) was collected from healthy controls, advanced HF patients at LVAD implantation and during follow-up at 3 and 6 months of LVAD support and at LVAD explantation, and from ambulatory stable HF patients. Parallel mRNA expression profiling using Illumina BeadChips (n=4 per condition) enabled analysis of miRNA seed-dependent target repression
studies.disease: Heart failure (advanced/end-stage and stable), including dilated cardiomyopathy and ischemic cardiomyopathy subtypes
studies.tissue: Left ventricular myocardium; circulating blood components (plasma and serum)
studies.sample_collection_method: Surgical procurement of myocardial tissue at LVAD implantation and during LVAD explantation at heart transplantation; fetal myocardium obtained after elective termination; venipuncture blood draw for potassium‑EDTA–treated plasma and serum

Plasma: 51 individuals (85 samples) comprising healthy controls (13 individuals, 17 samples), advanced HF (24 individuals, 54 samples) with timepoints at LVAD implantation, 3 months (10 samples), 6 months (10 samples), and LVAD explantation (7 samples), and stable HF (14 individuals, 14 samples). Serum: 18 individuals (18 samples) comprising healthy controls (4 individuals, 4 samples), advanced HF (7 individuals, 14 samples), and advanced HF at LVAD explantation (7 individuals, 7 samples)

studies.title: Cellular landscapes of non-diseased human cardiac valves from end-stage heart failure-explanted heart
studies.overall_design: Single-cell RNA-seq profiling of 18 human cardiac valve specimens from seven heart transplantation (HTx) recipients: five aortic valves, five pulmonary valves, five tricuspid valves, and three mitral valves. Valves were clinically and histologically determined to be non-diseased. Freshly collected valve leaflets were enzymatically digested to generate single-cell suspensions and sequenced using the 10x Genomics Chromium platform; reads were demultiplexed, mapped to GRCh38, and UMI-counted. Raw sequence data deposited at China Genomic Sequence Archive (GSA) under HRA002977

studies.tissue: Cardiac valve leaflets (aortic, pulmonary, tricuspid, mitral)

studies.title: Epigenetic regulation of transcription factor binding motifs promotes Th1 response in Chagas disease cardiomyopathy
studies.overall_design: Multi-cohort human study integrating bulk RNA-seq and genome-wide DNA methylation (MethylationEPIC) to characterize genetic and epigenetic deregulations in Chronic Chagasic Cardiomyopathy (CCC). Left ventricular free wall myocardium from severe end-stage CCC patients (n=8) obtained at heart transplantation was compared with healthy donor hearts (n=6) and DCM myocardium (n=8) by RNA-seq; the same heart tissue set underwent methylation profiling. Blood DNA methylation was also profiled in 96 CCC patients (48 moderate, EF>40%; 48 severe, EF<40%) and 48 asymptomatic Chagas controls. Differentially methylated regions and transcription factor binding sites were identified and integrated with gene expression to implicate immune/Th1, ion transport, cardiac muscle, and nervous system processes in CCC pathogenesis

studies.tissue: Human left ventricular free wall myocardium; peripheral blood

studies.title: High-content CRISPR screens link coronary artery disease genes to endothelial cell programs [Pilot scRNA-seq]
studies.overall_design: Pilot Perturb-seq studies in human immortalized endothelial cells engineered with doxycycline-inducible CRISPR interference (CRISPRi TeloHAEC and CRISPRi Eahy926). Cells were transduced with 50-gene or 200-gene promoter-targeting guide libraries in a CROP-seq vector. After 5 days of doxycycline induction, cells were captured on a 10x Chromium (standard 16k loading or ~150k superloading) for single-cell RNA-seq, and dialout libraries were generated to map guide sequences to scRNA-seq cell barcodes. Consensus NMF was used to define gene expression programs and link upstream regulators to programs
studies.tissue: Vascular endothelial cells; telomerase-immortalized human aortic endothelial cells (TeloHAEC) and Eahy926 endothelial hybrid line
studies.sample_collection_method: In vitro culture of human endothelial cell lines; pooled CRISPRi transduction; 10x Chromium single-cell RNA-seq with dialout libraries to assign guides to cells
studies.study_cohorts: - CRISPRi Eahy926 with 50-gene library, superloaded ~150K cells (three scRNA-seq runs) and one standard 16K lane, plus corresponding dialout libraries - CRISPRi TeloHAEC with 50-gene library, superloaded ~150K cells, plus dialout libraries - CRISPRi TeloHAEC with 200-gene library (two scRNA-seq runs) and corresponding dialout libraries
studies.total_samples: 15 sequencing libraries total (7 scRNA-seq libraries and 8 dialout libraries)
studies.clinical_endpoint: Gene expression changes and discovery of endothelial gene programs/regulators via CRISPRi-Perturb-seq
studies.treatment_type: Chemical induction (doxycycline) to activate CRISPRi machinery
studies.publication_title: Convergence of coronary artery disease genes onto endothelial cell programs

studies.public_datasets_used_for_validation: Human right coronary artery endothelial cell scRNA-seq (RCAEC) [Wirka et al. 2019] ; ENCODE datasets ENCSR000EVW (GATA2 ChIP-seq, HUVEC) and ENCSR000EOB (DNase/DGF, HMVEC) ; FANTOM5 expression atlas for endothelial specificity ; Endothelial snATAC-seq peaks for S-LDSC annotations

Analyses used a multi-analytic approach with covariate adjustment for age and sex to identify shared heart failure and etiology-specific signatures

studies.title: Spatial transcriptional landscape of human heart failure
studies.overall_design: Heart tissues from 44 participants with various cardiomyopathies and controls were processed into tissue microarrays (TMA). Ninety tissue cores spanning diverse histologies were selected, stained with Troponin I, Vimentin, and CD31, and profiled using the NanoString GeoMx Human Whole Transcriptome Atlas (WTA). Regions of interest were segmented to enrich cardiomyocytes, endothelial cells, and fibroblasts, yielding 178 areas of interest (AOIs). Data underwent QC, ComBat-seq batch correction, and normalization; cell-type deconvolution (SpatialDecon) leveraged external sc/snRNA-seq references. Differential expression was modeled with DREAM to account for repeated sampling within individuals. Findings were integrated with histologic grades (degeneration, fibrosis, hypertrophy, disarray) and clinical phenotypes to define compensated vs. uncompensated states. Validation included immunohistochemistry/multiplex IHC, external sc/snRNA-seq integration, and UK Biobank rare-variant analysis
studies.disease: Heart failure and cardiomyopathies (dilated cardiomyopathy, ischaemic cardiomyopathy, hypertrophic cardiomyopathy)
studies.tissue: Left ventricular myocardium (ventricular myocardium); RV segments included only for QC and excluded from downstream analyses
studies.sample_collection_method: Cardiac tissue obtained during surgical procedures (e.g., LVAD implantation and heart transplantation) across collection events; additional control tissues from non-cardiac death autopsies; processed into FFPE TMAs
studies.study_cohorts: Clinical phenotypes included DCM (n=9), ICM (n=10), HCM (n=18; HCMpEF n=9, HCMrEF n=9); controls (n=7) comprising non-cardiac deaths (n=4), isolated RV failure (n=1), and severe lung disease cases requiring heart–lung co-transplantation (n=2; one also with RV failure). RV segments were excluded from downstream analyses
studies.ejection_fraction_info: Mean LV ejection fraction: DCM 23%, ICM 19%, HCMrEF 36%, HCMpEF 66%. Additional LVEF metrics tabulated in Table 1; ACKR1/PLVAP-positive EC density associated with LVEF <30%
studies.total_samples: 90 tissue cores; 117 ROIs; 178 AOI segments profiled
studies.diseased_samples: 146 AOIs from diseased clinical phenotypes (DCM 40 + ICM 34 + HCM 72)
studies.healthy_control_samples: 11 AOIs labeled as Control in clinical phenotype table
studies.treated_samples: 28 AOIs from samples collected after LVAD ("After LVAD Yes 28")
studies.matched_controls: no (controls intentionally diverse rather than matched to diseased subjects)
studies.treated_patients: 7 patients received both LVAD and heart transplant sequentially; number with LVAD alone not specified
studies.clinical_endpoint: Cell-type- and histology-specific gene expression changes associated with HF progression and key histologic features (degeneration, fibrosis, hypertrophy, disarray), identification of biomarkers/subtypes, and delineation of compensated vs. uncompensated end-stage HF states
studies.comorbidities: Severe lung disease; isolated right ventricular (RV) failure
studies.treatment_type: Surgical/mechanical circulatory support (LVAD) and heart transplantation
studies.treatment_agent_name: Left ventricular assist device (LVAD); heart transplantation
studies.biological_replicates: yes (multiple tissue cores and AOIs per participant; DREAM used to adjust for repeated measures)

studies.overall_design: Transmural left ventricular (LV) apical/anterior wall tissue from donor hearts and patients with dilated (non-ischemic) cardiomyopathy was collected during donor procurement, LVAD implantation or heart transplantation, then processed for single-nucleus RNA-seq (snRNA-seq; n=38) or single-cell RNA-seq (scRNA-seq; n=7) using the 10X Genomics 5′ Single Cell platform; libraries were sequenced on an Illumina NovaSeq 6000 (~100,000 reads per cell/nucleus). Data from 45 individuals were integrated using Harmony/Seurat to define cell types and assess effects of disease, age, sex and severity on cellular composition and gene expression

studies.title: Integrated multiomics of pressure overload in the human heart prioritizes targets relevant to heart failure
studies.overall_design: Multi-layered human study integrating: (1) circulating proteomics in severe aortic stenosis (AS) to derive proteomic signatures of echocardiographic remodeling; (2) validation against cardiac MRI-derived interstitial fibrosis and functional capacity; (3) association with all-cause mortality and incident heart failure in an at-risk population (UK Biobank); (4) mapping of the remodeling proteome to myocardial single-nuclear transcriptomes from left ventricular tissue (AS vs unused donor hearts); and (5) genetic validation using transcriptome-wide and proteome-wide association studies to implicate causal targets in heart failure. Sampling included peripheral venous plasma prior to TAVI and left ventricular basal septal tissue obtained during SAVR or from unused donor hearts; snRNA-seq libraries prepared with 10x Genomics Multiome
studies.disease: Aortic stenosis (pressure overload) and heart failure susceptibility
studies.tissue: Left ventricular myocardium (basal interventricular septum); peripheral venous blood/plasma
studies.sample_collection_method: Myocardial tissue procured with a scalpel from the basal septum during surgical aortic valve replacement (SAVR) and from unused donor hearts; peripheral venous blood drawn prior to transcatheter aortic valve implantation (TAVI) for proteomics

studies.title: Platelet Reactivity ExpreSsion Score Predicts Cardiovascular Risk
studies.overall_design: Prospective cohort of symptomatic peripheral artery disease (PAD) patients undergoing nonemergent lower extremity revascularization (LER) in the PACE-PAD study. Immediately before LER, fasting peripheral blood was collected; platelet aggregation was measured by light transmission aggregometry (LTA) across multiple agonists with hyperreactivity defined as >60% aggregation to 0.4 µM epinephrine, and platelets were isolated for RNA sequencing. Differential expression and correlation analyses identified a 451-gene signature associated with hyperreactive platelets. A soft-voting ensemble machine-learning model using these genes generated a Platelet Reactivity ExpreSsion Score (PRESS), trained with 10-fold cross-validation, then refit and validated in an external healthy cohort. PRESS was further evaluated for association with acute MI and with future cardiovascular events. Platelet RNA-seq generated on Illumina NovaSeq 6000; reads aligned to hg38 with STAR and quantified with featureCounts. Data deposited to GEO
studies.tissue: Platelets (isolated from peripheral blood)
studies.sample_collection_method: Peripheral blood draw at onset of LER; citrate-anticoagulated, processed to platelet-rich plasma, followed by leukocyte and RBC depletion prior to RNA extraction
studies.study_cohorts: PACE-PAD: PAD patients undergoing LER (n=300 enrolled; 287 analyzed; platelet aggregation n=254; platelet RNA-seq n=129; PRESS derivation subset n=84). External validation: Duke healthy cohort off antiplatelet therapy (n=35). Additional validation cohorts with platelet RNA-seq: lower extremity atherosclerosis vs high-risk primary prevention (n=129 vs 28), systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE) vs controls (n=51 vs 18), COVID-19 vs controls (n=8 vs 10), reticulated vs non-reticulated platelets, and HARP women with acute MI (n=19) vs obstructive CAD without MI (n=9)
studies.clinical_endpoint: Primary 30-day composite of major adverse cardiac or limb events (MACLE: death, MI, stroke, major amputation, or acute limb ischemia/reintervention); longer-term composite of major adverse cardiovascular events (MACE: death, MI, stroke, major amputation) over median ~18 months in RNA-seq subset
studies.comorbidities: High prevalence of coronary artery disease (54%), diabetes mellitus (53%), and critical limb ischemia (78%). Carotid artery disease and prior stroke considered in multivariable models
studies.publication_title: A Platelet Reactivity ExpreSsion Score derived from patients with peripheral artery disease predicts cardiovascular risk
studies.data_availability: Platelet RNA-seq data deposited in GEO (GSE232027); additional public data GSE226147, GSE126448, GSE176480; source data provided with the paper; other data available upon request
studies.author: Jeffrey S. Berger
studies.orthogonal_validation: Validated against platelet function by light transmission aggregometry (association of hyperreactivity >60% epinephrine 0.4 µM with MACLE); external validation in an independent healthy cohort; risk stratification for MI and future CV events
studies.public_datasets_used_for_validation: GSE226147; GSE126448; GSE176480; additional validation on published reticulated vs non-reticulated platelet transcriptomes and cohorts including atherosclerosis, SLE, and COVID-19
